description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern of alternating stone and recessed dark gaps. The grooves are deep and uniform, giving the block a visually striking, ribbed appearance. The stone material appears to be a rough, speckled composite, possibly granite or concrete, with a natural, unpolished finish. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a heavy-duty barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended for use in areas requiring both durability and drainage, such as sidewalks, plazas, or public parks.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top, creating a striped, ribbed surface. The stone is rough and speckled, with solid, unadorned sides.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the deep, parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's thickness clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern of grooves and the solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the uniformity and depth of the grooves across the top surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza drainage barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some minor surface discoloration and speckling, suggesting exposure to the elements but no significant erosion or chipping.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with or adjacent to pavement or other stone blocks
